calendar.matters:
PROBATE EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER TO DETERMINE AND APPROVE FINALITY OF FAMILY LAW JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-30-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Gary Rothstein, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Suzzan Eways, Petitioner.
Paul Epstein, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Susan D. Nazzal, Creditor.
Alex Verbeck, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, specially appearing for and with Jihad Nazzal, in person, Heir.
Hadeel Abutouk, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, attorney for Suzan D. Nazzal in related family court matter.

At the outset, Mr. Verbeck advises of his intent to represent Mr. Nazzal but has not filed anything in this matter. The court acknowledges Mr. Verbeck's notice to the court. The court confirms with Mr. Nazzal that Mr. Verbeck may represent him at this hearing. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the instant petition and the related creditor's judgment arising from the related family law matter. Mr. Rothstein believes that the only remaining issue before closing the estate is the creditor's judgment. There is discussion regarding the judgment and Mr. Verbeck's plan to seek relief.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission with the intent to grant as prayed.

Judge: Hon. Lydia Villarreal. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Wong. Not Reported.
